Feedback? Ideas? Complaints? Suggestions? Request for report about other area? send me a message!
This page was generated using data obtained on various dates including 2023-04-02, 2023-04-03. Please, send me a message if you want it updated!




Remember to check whether edits make sense! All reports are on this page because these tasks require human judgment to verify whether the proposed edit makes sense. Some proposed edits will be wrong, in such case they should be not done and problem reported

wikipedia tag links to 404
Wikipedia article linked from OSM object using wikipedia tag is missing.
Often article was moved without leaving redirect and wikipedia tag should be edited to point to the new one.
Article may be deleted and no longer existing, or link was never valid. In such cases wikipedia tag should be deleted.

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a violation of law
linked wikidata entry (Q878856) is about a violation of law, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
Hinterkaifeck-Marterl - an affected OSM element that may be improved

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident
linked wikidata entry (Q2254414) is about a transport accident, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a human
linked wikidata entry (Q1706783) is about a human, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or name:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an animal or plant (and not an individual one)
linked wikidata entry (Q12004) is about an animal or plant (and not an individual one), so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
an affected OSM element that may be improved

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a vehicle model or class
linked wikidata entry (Q733356) is about a vehicle model or class, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or model:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not
wikidata and wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, wikidata does not. wikidata tag is likely to be correct, wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a software
linked wikidata entry (Q3285992) is about a software, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a train category
linked wikidata entry (Q680235) is about a train category, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
Wackelstein - an affected OSM element that may be improved

overpass query usable in JOSM that will load all objects where this specific error is present:

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for brand prefixed tags
brand:wikidata and brand:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because brand:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, brand:wikidata does not. brand:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, brand:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a sports competition
linked wikidata entry (Q1060073) is about a sports competition, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

subject:wikipedia=* or brand:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

no longer existing brand (according to Wikidata)
Wikidata claims that assigned brand object no longer exists. That means that either this shop is gone or it is rebranded. Or brand:wikidata tag is wrong. Historical, no longer existing object should not be mapped in OSM. REMEMBER TO VERIFY! WIKIDATA QUALITY MAY BE POOR! WIKIDATA MUST NOT BE USED AS SOURCE! ALWAYS VERIFY!

no longer existing object (according to Wikidata)
Wikidata claims that this object no longer exists. Historical, no longer existing object should not be mapped in OSM (except temporary marking to avoid remapping them from aerial imagery or similar sources) - so it means that either Wikidata is mistaken or has only partial data - for example it is fine to link ruins of a church to its wikipedia entry ( see https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata:Project_chat&oldid=1361617968#Tagging_ruins/remains_left_after_object ) or wikipedia/wikidata tag is wrong or OSM has an outdated object that should be removed. REMEMBER TO VERIFY! WIKIDATA QUALITY MAY BE POOR! WIKIDATA MUST NOT BE USED AS SOURCE! ALWAYS VERIFY!
